source.title: Pain affect encoded in human anterior cingulate but not somatosensory cortex.

confidence_rationale: Canonical human PET study using hypnotic modulation to selectively alter pain unpleasantness while holding intensity constant. Demonstrates functional dissociation between ACC (affect) and S1 (intensity/sensory-discriminative), directly instantiating the two-stream split that SD-011 encodes as z_harm_a vs z_harm_s. High source quality (Science, >5000 citations), clean experimental design, direct mapping to claim.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Anterior cingulate cortex encodes pain affect (unpleasantness) selectively; primary somatosensory cortex encodes pain intensity. Hypnotic modulation of unpleasantness changes ACC activity without changing S1 activity.
mapping.ree_translation: ACC activation maps onto z_harm_a (affective-motivational stream): encodes how unpleasant harm is, drives avoidance priority. S1/S2 activation maps onto z_harm_s (sensory-discriminative stream): encodes where/what/how intense the harm signal is. The experimental dissociation is the strongest human evidence that these are genuinely separable representational channels.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The ACC/S1 dissociation is cortical — it does not directly demonstrate the upstream lateral vs. medial spinothalamic split that is the proposed anatomical basis of SD-011. The cortical readout could in principle arise from a single upstream stream with divergent projections rather than two anatomically distinct ascending pathways. SD-011 posits stream separation earlier (z_harm_s / z_harm_a as latent variables), so the cortical dissociation is consistent but not proof of the upstream split.
mapping.source_context: Human adults, acute experimental pain (hot water immersion), hypnotic suggestion to modulate unpleasantness, PET neuroimaging

confidence_components.notes: Transfer risk is low: human study with direct relevance to the proposed cortical-level representational distinction. The main risk is that cortical dissociation may not require the subcortical stream separation that SD-011 specifically encodes. This does not weaken support for the functional claim, only for the precise anatomical mechanism.
